commands.name: bitmaps
commands.description: TIFF (.tif/.tiff) images can be compiled into a bitmap using the bitmaps verb:

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to a folder containing a set of images for processing.

commands.name: build-noise-map
commands.description: This command generates random noise like you would see in an image editor. The result can then be converted into a bitmap tag or taken to your image editing software to tweak further.

args.name: tiff-file
args.description: A local data path to where the generated tiff will be placed. Make sure to include filename and extension.

args.name: width
args.description: The width of the image with a max range of 16-4096. Value should be a power of 2 dimension

args.name: height
args.description: The height of the image with a max range of 16-4096. Value should be a power of 2 dimension

args.name: low-freq-skip
args.description: Low-frequency "octaves" to skip. A higher value means more noise

args.name: high-freq-skip
args.description: High-frequency "octaves" to skip. A higher value more blur

args.name: roughness
args.description: How smooth or rough the image is with a range of 0.0-1.0. With 0.0 being the smoothest image possible and 1.0 being the roughest image possible.

args.name: blur-passes
args.description: The number of times to apply a blur pass.

args.name: random-seed
args.description: A random seed for noise.

commands.name: lightmaps-farm-merge
commands.description: Merge the results from lightmaps-farm-worker to form the final lightmap bitmap tag.

args.name: scenario
args.description: A local tag path to a scenario tag without extension.

args.name: bsp-name
args.description: The name of a scenario_structure_bsp tag without extension and belongs to the referenced scenario.

args.name: worker-count
args.description: The number of lightmaps-farm-worker instances that were used.

commands.name: build-cache-file
commands.description: A scenario can be compiled into a map using the build-cache-file verb. Simply provide your scenario's tag path. (the last two arguments are optional):

args.name: scenario
args.description: A local tag path to your scenario without the file extension

commands.name: bulk-import-crates
commands.description: Compile multiple source directories in a single run to generate a crate tag and all the relevant tags involved.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMI file is located.

commands.name: bulk-import-scenery
commands.description: Compile multiple source directories in a single run to generate a scenery tag and all the relevant tags involved.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMI file is located.

commands.name: export-tag-to-xml
commands.description: Dumps the tag values set in the tag to an XML format to easily compare in text editors.

args.name: tag-file
args.description: An absolute tag path with extension.

args.name: output-file
args.description: A absolute path with file name and XML extension to set where to write the XML to.

commands.name: export-tags-to-xml
commands.description: Exports multiple tags to multiple XML files in a single run to easily compare in text editors. Files will get dumped to the root of your H2-EK root unless you give an absolute path

args.name: export-tag-to-xml-params-listing
args.description: An absolute file path to a text file containing a list of tags to export. Sample file below.

commands.name: export-windows-font
commands.description: Given a system font, this verb generates .tif files for each of its glyphs within a subdirectory of data.

args.name: output-directory
args.description: The name of the directory within data where tif files will be generated to. Tool expects that this directory and a subdirectory. called exported alread exist. For an argument of example, Tool would expect to find the directory data\example\exported.

args.name: font-file
args.description: While this argument is marked optional, it appears to be required to make the command work but is possibly unused. Use any string.

args.name: typeface-name
args.description: The name of the system font to be exported, e.g. "Arial Bold".

args.name: point-size
args.description: The font size to generate glyphs at.

commands.name: import-decorator-set
commands.description: Imports a JMI set to generate a decorator_set tag.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to a JMI file.

commands.name: bulk-collision
commands.description: Multiple directories can have their collision files compiled in a single run using this command.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMI file is located.

commands.name: bulk-import-crates-folder
commands.description: Generates a crate tag and compiles the render, collision, and physics directory of a model in a single run. Just point it at the root of your model folder and go!

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.name: bulk-import-model-folder
commands.description: Generates a model tag and compiles the render, collision, and physics directory of a model in a single run. Just point it at the root of your model folder and go!

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.name: bulk-import-models
commands.description: Compile multiple source directories in a single run to generate a model tag and all the relevant tags involved.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMI file is located.

commands.name: bulk-import-scenery-folder
commands.description: Generates a scenery tag and compiles the render, collision, and physics directory of a model in a single run. Just point it at the root of your model folder and go!

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.name: bulk-physics
commands.description: Multiple directories can have their physics files compiled in a single run using this command.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMI file is located.

commands.name: bulk-render
commands.description: Multiple directories can have their render files compiled in a single run using this command.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMI file is located.

commands.name: camera-track
commands.description: This commands takes a JMA file directly and converts it to a camera_track for the game. The length of the animation should be at most 16 as that is the max number of control points a camera track tag can contain. The JMA should contain a skeleton made up of a single bone that will change its position each frame. The orientation of the bone will determine the position and rotation of the control point for that index. The name of the JMA file will be the name of the tag.

args.name: source-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMA file is located.

commands.name: collision
commands.description: A JMS file containing a collision model can be compiled into a collision model tag

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.name: extract-collision-data
commands.description: Extracts import info from a collision_model tag to retrieve the original JMS used to create the tag. Don't expect this command to do anything if there is no import info.

args.name: collision_model
args.description: A local tag path to a collision tag without file extension.

commands.name: extract-physics-data
commands.description: Extracts import info from a physics_model tag to retrieve the original JMS used to create the tag. Don't expect this command to do anything if there is no import info.

args.name: physics_model
args.description: A local tag path to a physics_model tag without extension.

commands.name: extract-render-data
commands.description: Extracts import info from a render_model tag to retrieve the original JMS used to create the tag. Don't expect this command to do anything if there is no import info.

args.name: render_model
args.description: A local tag path to a render_model tag without extension.

commands.name: extract-structure-data
commands.description: Extracts import info from a scenario_structure_bsp tag to retrieve the original ASS or JMS used to create the tag. Don't expect this command to do anything if there is no import info stored in the BSP tag.

args.name: scenario_structure_bsp
args.description: A local tag path to a scenario_structure_bsp tag without extension.

commands.name: fbx-to-ass
commands.description: This command takes an FBX and converts it to an ASS file for Halo 2 level importing. Use this if you don't have access to an export script.

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: ass
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: fbx-to-jma
commands.description: This command takes an FBX and converts it to an animation source file for Halo 2 importing. Use this if you don't have access to an export script. Be aware that the extension can be any of the available extensions for animation importing. It does not specifically needs to be JMA. You can type JMO as the extension and the output is still valid.

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: jma
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: fbx-to-jmi
commands.description: This command takes an FBX and converts it to a JMI source file for Halo 2 importing. Use this if you don't have access to an export script.

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: jmi
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: fbx-to-jms
commands.description: FBX to JMS

args.name: render,collision,physics-or-all
args.description: Sets the type of geo this FBX is for. Use either render, collision, physics, or all only. The geo class set will determine what geometry gets exported from the JMS

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: jms
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: fix-model-materials
commands.description: Sets global material names for model tags in a directory if the name is invalid.

args.name: root-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory containing model tags.

args.name: material-name
args.description: What to set the global material name to.

commands.name: fp-model-animations
commands.description: Import animation source files as a first person animation. Use this if you're making FP weapon animations.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

args.name: character-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of the first person character model source directory the weapon is using.

args.name: weapon-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of the first person weapon model source directory the character is using.

commands.name: import-particle-model
commands.description: Imports a JMI set to generate a particle tag.

args.name: jmi-file
args.description: A local data path to a JMI file.

commands.name: model-animation-count
commands.description: Goes to all the animations in a directory and child directories. Once it's done it prints debug info.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory containing model_animation_graph tags or child folders with model_animation_graph tags.

commands.name: model-animation-status
commands.description: Goes to all the animations in a directory and child directories. Once it's done it prints debug info.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory containing model_animation_graph tags or child folders with model_animation_graph tags.

commands.name: model-animations
commands.description: A set of animation source files can be compiled into a model_animation_graph tag.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.name: physics
commands.description: A JMS file containing physic meshes can be compiled into a physics_model tag

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.name: prt-simulation
commands.description: Run a PRT (precomputed radiance transfer) simulation on an already existing render_model tag.

args.name: render-model
args.description: A local tag path to a render_model tag without extension.

commands.name: render
commands.description: A JMS file containing render geometry can be compiled into a render_model tag

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.name: structure
commands.description: A JMS file containing level geometry can be compiled into a scenario_structure_bsp tag. Do not use this command for level imports as it's outdated (use structure-new-from-ass instead).

args.name: scenario-directory
args.description: A local data path to root directory of a level.

args.name: bsp-name
args.description: The JMS filename without extension.

commands.name: structure-compatible
commands.description: A JMS file containing level geometry can be compiled into a scenario_structure_bsp tag. Do not use this command for level imports as it's outdated (use structure-new-from-ass instead).

args.name: scenario-directory
args.description: A local data path to root directory of a level.

args.name: bsp-name
args.description: The JMS filename without extension.

commands.name: structure-compatible-from-jms
commands.description: A JMS file containing level geometry can be compiled into a scenario_structure_bsp tag. Do not use this command for level imports as it's outdated (use structure-new-from-ass instead).

args.name: jms-file
args.description: A local data path to a JMS file with extension.

commands.name: structure-from-jms
commands.description: A JMS file containing level geometry can be compiled into a scenario_structure_bsp tag. Do not use this command for level imports as it's outdated (use structure-new-from-ass instead).

args.name: jms-file

commands.name: structure-new
commands.description: A ASS file containing level geometry can be compiled into a scenario_structure_bsp tag. Do not use this command for level imports as it's outdated (use structure-new-from-ass instead).

args.name: scenario-directory
args.description: A local data path to root directory of a level.

args.name: bsp-name
args.description: The ASS filename without extension.

commands.name: structure-new-from-ass
commands.description: A ASS file containing level geometry can be compiled into a scenario_structure_bsp tag.

args.name: ass-file
args.description: A local data path to a ASS file with extension.

commands.name: structure-new-verbose-from-ass
commands.description: A ASS file containing level geometry can be compiled into a scenario_structure_bsp tag. This version of the structure command gives verbose output.

args.name: ass-file
args.description: A local data path to a ASS file with extension.

commands.name: count-all-class-sounds
commands.description: Goes through all of the sound tags in a directory and prints how many sounds belong to a specific class

args.name: root-path
args.description: A local tag path to the root of a directory containing sound tags or child folders with sound tags.

commands.name: count-class-sounds
commands.description: Goes through all of the sound tags in the tags directory and prints the paths for sounds that use a specific sound class

args.name: sound-class
args.description: The sound class you want to specify. The list is as follows.Placeholder

commands.name: count-sound-tags
commands.description: Prints all existing sound tags in a directory.

args.name: root-path
args.description: A local tag path to the root of a directory containing sound tags or child folders with sound tags.

commands.name: dialogue-import
commands.description: Collects all sound tag paths and adds them to a generated dialogue tag.

args.name: root-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a root directory containing sound tags. A dialogue tag is generated adjacent to this root directory with the same name, e.g. sound\dialog\combat\brute_bloodthirsty.dialogue.

commands.name: dump-uncompressed-sounds
commands.description: Prints the paths of sound tags that use none (little endian) as their compression format.

args.name: root-path
args.description: A local tag path to the root of a directory containing sound tags.

commands.name: extract-lpc-data
commands.description: Dumps a file called test.aif in the root of H2-EK. This is likely to be linear predictive coding data for the sound file but this hasn't be verified.

args.name: sound-file
args.description: A local data path to an AIF/AIFF file.

commands.name: mission-dialogue-import
commands.description: Gathers all sound tags in a directory to generate an ai_mission_dialogue tag

args.name: root-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory containing a cinematic and mission folder that contain sound files.

commands.name: reimport-sounds
commands.description: Goes through all sound tags in a directory or child directories. Any sound tags that are found will have their sound files from data reimported.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory or child directory containing sound tags.

commands.name: reimport-sounds-single
commands.description: Reimports the specified sound if sound data was found in the data folder.

args.name: source-file
args.description: A local tag path to a sound tag without extension.

commands.name: reimport-sounds-to-opus
commands.description: Goes through all sound tags in a directory or child directories. Any sound tags that are found will have their sound files from data reimported. Sounds reimported using this command will use Opus for their compression setting.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory or child directory containing sound tags.

commands.name: reimport-sounds-to-opus-single
commands.description: Reimports the specified sound if sound data was found in the data folder. Sounds reimported using this command will use Opus for their compression setting.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory or child directory containing sound tags.

commands.name: set-sound-class
commands.description: Grabs all the sound tags in a directory and changes the class to whatever was specified by the user.

args.name: source-directory

args.name: new-sound-class
args.description: A sound class string.

commands.name: sound-multi-layer
commands.description: Imports sound files in a directory with the import type set to multi-layer. All sound files the source directory and child directories will be combined into one sound file.

args.name: type
args.description: Set the sound class

args.name: type

commands.name: extract-unicode-strings
commands.description: Extract strings from multilingual_unicode_string_list and dumps the results back to a text file in data. Results may not match original source exactly so give the files a read to make sure tool won't have any issues.

args.name: multilingual_unicode_string_list
args.description: A local tag path to a multilingual_unicode_string_list tag without extension.

commands.name: new-strings
commands.description: Converts all text files in a directory to multilingual_unicode_string_list tags. Text files should be saved as UTF-16

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to a directory containing text files.

commands.name: pack-unicode-strings
commands.description: This command generates the STR files found in the font folder. These files contain the string data the game reads to display text ingame.
commands.example: tool pack-unicode-strings en "F:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Halo MCCEK\Halo Assets\2\Vanilla\data\strings_list.txt"

args.name: language
args.description: The language you are compiling a STR file for. The following the the list of languages currently used by MCC.chs

args.name: tag-list-file
args.description: An absolute file path to a text file containing a list of multilingual_unicode_string_list tags to include in the packaged STR file.

commands.name: extract-hs-scripts
commands.description: Finds all scenario_hs_source_file tags in a directory and dumps the contents to an .hsc file in data.

args.name: name_substring
args.description: A local tag path to a directory containing scenario_hs_source_file tag files.

args.description: A local tag path to a scenario tag without extension.

args.name: bsp-name
args.description: The name of a scenario_structure_bsp tag without extension and belongs to the referenced scenario.

args.name: quality-setting
args.description: Standard Halo 2 light quality string. The list of options is as follows.checkerboard

commands.description: A command to debug lightmaps. Debug them how you ask? That's an excellent question! Lets move on to the next command.

args.name: scenario

commands.name: dialogue-globals-import
commands.description: Compiles a CSV file to generate a new ai_dialogue_globals tag. The file path for the CSV file is data\ai\ai_dialogue_globals.csv and tags\ai\ai_dialogue_globals.ai_dialogue_globals is generated.

commands.name: fix-tags
commands.description: Various command to fix up tags. Almost all of them are meant for porting Xbox to PC.

args.name: action
args.description: What action to take. Type something invalid like "help" to see a full list of options.

commands.name: import-damage-table
commands.description: Imports data\globals\armor_vs_damage.csv to update the damage table block in globals. The CSV (which can be exported from any spreadsheet software like Excel) uses Damage Groups as the columns, and Armor Modifiers as the rows, with each cell being the applicable Damage Multiplier.

args.name: scenario

args.description: Standard Halo 2 light quality string. The list of options is as follows.checkerboard

args.name: model-animation-graph
args.description: Argument documented by the command syntax.

commands.name: monitor-bitmaps
commands.description: Keeps watch of the data directory for any file changes. If any tiff places are modified or placed in the data directory then tool will immediately attempt to import the image file as bitmap tags.

commands.name: monitor-data-and-tags-bitmaps
commands.description: Keeps watch of the data and tags directory for any file changes. If any tiff or tags are modified or placed in the data or tags directory then tool will immediately attempt to import the image file as bitmap tags.

commands.name: monitor-models
commands.description: Keeps watch of the data directory for any file changes. If any model related intermediate files are modified or moved to the data directory then tool will immediately attempt to import the source files as render_model, collision_model, or physics_model tags.

commands.name: monitor-structures
commands.description: Keeps watch of the data directory for any file changes. If any level related intermediate files are modified or moved to the data directory then tool will immediately attempt to import the source files as levels.

commands.name: plate
commands.description: Gather images in a directory and set up a plate with all the images placed properly. Useful for creating animated textures from multiple images easily.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: An absolute file path to a directory containing image files.

args.name: structure
args.description: A local tag path to a scenario_structure_bsp tag without extension.

commands.name: script-doc
commands.description: Prints the script_doc or any command that contains the given string.

args.name: scenario-directory
args.description: A local data path to root directory of a level.

args.name: bsp-name
args.description: The ASS filename without extension.

args.name: tag-file
args.description: Argument documented by the command syntax.

commands.description: Checks if a tag is valid

commands.name: version
commands.description: Outputs the build version for tool.exe
